verses.text: Still imprisoned, Jeremiah hears, 'Call unto me, and I will answer thee, and shew thee great and mighty things, which thou knowest not.' God invites prayer. He promises to reveal great things to those who call on Him.

Still imprisoned, Jeremiah hears, 'Call unto me, and I will answer thee, and shew thee great and mighty things, which thou knowest not.' God invites prayer. He promises to reveal great things to those who call on Him.

Still imprisoned, Jeremiah hears, 'Call unto me, and I will answer thee, and shew thee great and mighty things, which thou knowest not.' God invites prayer. He promises to reveal great things to those who call on Him.

Of the houses torn down for defence, God says He will yet 'bring it health and cure,' revealing 'the abundance of peace and truth.' Healing is promised. The God who wounded the city will restore its peace.

Of the houses torn down for defence, God says He will yet 'bring it health and cure,' revealing 'the abundance of peace and truth.' Healing is promised. The God who wounded the city will restore its peace.

Of the houses torn down for defence, God says He will yet 'bring it health and cure,' revealing 'the abundance of peace and truth.' Healing is promised. The God who wounded the city will restore its peace.
He will cleanse them from all iniquity and pardon them, making the city 'a name of joy' before the nations. Pardon brings renown. God's mercy to His people becomes a witness to the world.
He will cleanse them from all iniquity and pardon them, making the city 'a name of joy' before the nations. Pardon brings renown. God's mercy to His people becomes a witness to the world.
He will cleanse them from all iniquity and pardon them, making the city 'a name of joy' before the nations. Pardon brings renown. God's mercy to His people becomes a witness to the world.

In the desolate places shall again be heard 'the voice of joy, and the voice of the bridegroom,' and flocks shall pass under the shepherd's counting hand. Life returns. God refills the silent land with gladness and plenty.

In the desolate places shall again be heard 'the voice of joy, and the voice of the bridegroom,' and flocks shall pass under the shepherd's counting hand. Life returns. God refills the silent land with gladness and plenty.

In the desolate places shall again be heard 'the voice of joy, and the voice of the bridegroom,' and flocks shall pass under the shepherd's counting hand. Life returns. God refills the silent land with gladness and plenty.

In the desolate places shall again be heard 'the voice of joy, and the voice of the bridegroom,' and flocks shall pass under the shepherd's counting hand. Life returns. God refills the silent land with gladness and plenty.

He will fulfil His promise: 'the Branch of righteousness to grow up unto David,' and the city shall be called, 'The LORD our righteousness.' This is Christ. The righteous King secures His people's salvation.

He will fulfil His promise: 'the Branch of righteousness to grow up unto David,' and the city shall be called, 'The LORD our righteousness.' This is Christ. The righteous King secures His people's salvation.

He will fulfil His promise: 'the Branch of righteousness to grow up unto David,' and the city shall be called, 'The LORD our righteousness.' This is Christ. The righteous King secures His people's salvation.

David shall never lack a man on the throne, nor the Levites a man to offer; the kingly and priestly lines endure. These point to Christ, King and Priest for ever. In Him both promises are eternally kept.

David shall never lack a man on the throne, nor the Levites a man to offer; the kingly and priestly lines endure. These point to Christ, King and Priest for ever. In Him both promises are eternally kept.
As the host of heaven and the sand of the sea cannot be numbered, so God will multiply David's seed and the Levites. The promise is vast. God's covenant fruitfulness exceeds all counting.

Though men say God has cast off the two families, His covenant with day and night stands, and so does His covenant with Jacob's seed. His word is sure. The God of the unfailing seasons will not abandon His people.
